reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 1 (Dead Code): PASSED — VerifyDataColumnSidecar is called from beacon-chain/verification/data_column.go:125 in ValidFields(), which is part of GossipDataColumnSidecarRequirements, ByRangeRequestDataColumnSidecarRequirements, and ByRootRequestDataColumnSidecarRequirements. Non-test callers exist. Gate 2 (Trust Boundary): PASSED — This validation runs on P2P gossip input (untrusted). Gate 3 (Code Verification): PASSED — The code at beacon-chain/core/peerdas/p2p_interface.go:34-52 factually checks len(kzg_commitments) > 0 (L42-43) and length consistency (L47-48), but does NOT validate len(kzg_commitments) <= max_blobs_per_block for the sidecar's epoch. Phase 03's code reading is accurate. Gate 4 (Exploitability): PASSED — An attacker can trigger this by gossiping a data column sidecar with excessive KZG commitments (e.g., 100 commitments when max_blobs_per_block is 6/9/12). The sidecar passes VerifyDataColumnSidecar and proceeds to KZG batch verification, causing memory and CPU exhaustion. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): 01e property PROP-6a4369e9-inv-009 from outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json (L227-242) asserts 'len(sidecar.kzg_commitments) <= get_blob_parameters(epoch).max_blobs_per_block', severity HIGH, covers SG-002. Gate 6 (Scope Check): PASSED — PeerDAS gossip validation is in-scope; not excluded by BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json. An attacker can trigger this via P2P gossip by sending data column sidecars with kzg_commitments count exceeding the epoch-dependent max_blobs_per_block limit (6 at Deneb, 9 at Electra, 12 at Fulu, or variable per EIP-7892 blob schedule), causing all Prysm nodes to process and store oversized columns, leading to memory exhaustion and potential DoS affecting the Prysm client (31% of CL network).
reviewed_items.spec_reference: kzg_commitments count must not exceed max_blobs_per_block for the sidecar's epoch. (forall sidecar: len(sidecar.kzg_commitments) <= get_blob_parameters(epoch).max_blobs_per_block)
